danger_signals.visual_form: Robot soldiers and hostile creatures on-screen
danger_signals.meaning: Combat encounter imminent; player must engage or avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Red flowers visible in environment or held by NPCs
danger_signals.meaning: Frenzying agent present; Mimigas in proximity may become violent

danger_signals.visual_form: HUD display shows weapon level decreasing after taking damage
Health bar in top-left HUD depleting or flashing red
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger to player character; death risk escalating

danger_signals.visual_form: Misery and Balrog appear in scene

safety_signals.visual_form: Peaceful rabbit-like NPCs in settled areas
safety_signals.meaning: Sanctuary zone; community refuge from military threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Experience point drops glowing on ground
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to collect; weapon progression available; no immediate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Health restore items appearing from defeated enemies
Old witch guardian in protective spaces
safety_signals.meaning: Resistance presence; sanctuary from Doctor's forces

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armed robot soldiers with distinct mechanical design; Misery and Balrog as distinctive commanders
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Industrial/mechanical areas; presence indicates military occupation zones

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Frenzied Mimiga (transformed by red flower consumption) versus peaceful Mimiga
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mimiga Village as settlement hub; Grasstown as inhabited zone; presence indicates civilian/resistance territory

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Professor Booster, Sakamoto family, scientific party
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Corrupted expedition members (Doctor, Fuyuhiko Date) versus allied researchers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Research facilities; areas containing red flower cultivation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Quote identified as 'soldier from the surface'; indicates shared military origin with current antagonists
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cave interior and island locations where military campaign occurred

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mimiga NPCs present in distressed states; villages under threat; kidnapping events (Toroko abduction)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Peaceful Mimiga flee or hide; King enforces punitive measures; Sue undertakes rescue missions

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Doctor's red flower army plan underway
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red flowers present in environment; flowers stored in warehouse; Jenka guards them
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs discuss threat of frenzied Mimiga army; Balrog actively steals flower warehouse key; player tasked with destruction

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Demon Crown sought by multiple factions
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Crown artifact concealed on island; evidence of previous military campaigns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Doctor commands expeditions seeking it; Fuyuhiko Date has acquired it; factions pursue control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Quote wakes in cave with no context; encounters Curly Brace (another amnesiac robot)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs gradually reveal player is 'soldier from the surface'; dual nature as potential enemy/ally emerges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cavernous interior environments; Sky Dragon egg as escape mechanism sought
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sue pursues dragon egg for island escape; stranded expedition members adapt to island

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Equipped weapon display in HUD; weapon level indicator (0-3); experience crystals drop from enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player grows stronger through combat; damage taken causes weapon regression; progression/regression cycle visible in HUD

environmental_storytelling.example: Mimiga village settlement conveys peaceful culture; kidnapped/transformed Mimigas show Doctor's persecution; Sue's presence in Egg Corridor reveals her sanctuary seeking
Mimiga Village represents native sanctuary; Sand Zone contains Jenka's flower guardianship; Grasstown shows civilian settlement; industrial/mechanical areas indicate military occupation
Red flowers visible convey threat system; Demon Crown as artifact drives faction motivation; Sky Dragon egg represents escape hope
Toroko kidnapping and mistaken identity shows Doctor's persecution; Curly Brace's amnesia mirrors player's condition; frenzied Mimigas demonstrate red flower effect on behavior
Weapon level restrictions prevent access to new areas; experience crystal collection tied to enemy encounters reveals combat-to-progress loop
Jenka's exposition of past robot military campaign establishes world history; player identified as surface soldier recontextualizes character role

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Inhabit settlements; interact peacefully with player; provide information and quests
Become hostile combatants; attack indiscriminately; pose threat to player and peaceful Mimigas
Patrol occupied territories; engage player in combat; follow Doctor's commands
Search for Sue Sakamoto; abduct Mimiga; steal critical resources (warehouse key); actively pursue Doctor's objectives
Directs persecution; plans red flower army creation; seeks Demon Crown; controls island's military infrastructure
Seeks rescue of brother Kazuma; pursues Sky Dragon egg as escape; wrongly blamed for Toroko kidnapping
Awakens in cave; gains weapon upgrades; progresses through platforming challenges; gradually learns identity as surface soldier
Encountered in Sand Zone; shares memory loss with player; indicates multiple robot entities with severed histories
Guards red flowers; opposes Doctor's plan; provides sanctuary and exposition; acts as protective maternal figure
Captures Sue; holds her responsible for Toroko's kidnapping; enforces village order despite persecution

notes: Cave Story uses a dual-layer visual communication system: the HUD conveys immediate player state (health, weapon level, experience points) while environmental design and NPC distribution convey broader world state (faction control, persecution, sanctuary). Danger signals are conveyed through enemy presence and weapon degradation mechanics; safety signals through NPC interaction availability and resource drops. Faction identity relies on visual character design (Mimigas as distinct rabbit-like creatures, robots as mechanical soldiers) and environmental association (settlements vs. military zones). The game communicates political tension through NPC dialogue, possession of key artifacts (red flowers, Demon Crown), and control of territory. World state shifts are visualized through NPC behavioral changes, character transformation (frenzied Mimigas), and environmental modifications (flower warehouse, dragon egg availability).
sources_used: wikipedia - Cave Story article (gameplay, plot, setting, story sections)
